# frozen_string_literal: true

class PostStatusService < BaseService
  include Redisable
  include Lockable
  include LanguagesHelper

  # How much to delay sending an e-mail about a new post, to allow grouping multiple posts
  EMAIL_DISTRIBUTION_DELAY = 5.minutes.freeze

  # If the job is not executed within this timeframe, it will lose its arguments
  EMAIL_DISTRIBUTION_TTL = 1.hour.to_i

  class UnexpectedMentionsError < StandardError
    attr_reader :accounts

    def initialize(message, accounts)
      super(message)
      @accounts = accounts
    end
  end

  # Post a text status update, fetch and notify remote users mentioned
  # @param [Account] account Account from which to post
  # @param [Hash] options
  # @option [String] :text Message
  # @option [Status] :thread Optional status to reply to
  # @option [Status] :quoted_status Optional status to quote
  # @option [String] :quote_approval_policy Approval policy for quotes, one of `public`, `followers` or `nobody`
  # @option [Boolean] :sensitive
  # @option [String] :visibility
  # @option [String] :spoiler_text
  # @option [String] :language
  # @option [String] :scheduled_at
  # @option [Hash] :poll Optional poll to attach
  # @option [Enumerable] :media_ids Optional array of media IDs to attach
  # @option [Doorkeeper::Application] :application
  # @option [String] :idempotency Optional idempotency key
  # @option [Boolean] :with_rate_limit
  # @option [Enumerable] :allowed_mentions Optional array of expected mentioned account IDs, raises `UnexpectedMentionsError` if unexpected accounts end up in mentions
  # @return [Status]
  def call(account, options = {})
    @account     = account
    @options     = options
    @text        = @options[:text] || ''
    @in_reply_to = @options[:thread]
    @quoted_status = @options[:quoted_status]

    with_idempotency do
      validate_media!
      preprocess_attributes!

      if scheduled?
        schedule_status!
      else
        process_status!
      end
    end

    unless scheduled?
      postprocess_status!
      bump_potential_friendship!
    end

    @status
  rescue Antispam::SilentlyDrop => e
    e.status
  end

  private

  def preprocess_attributes!
    @sensitive    = (@options[:sensitive].nil? ? @account.user&.setting_default_sensitive : @options[:sensitive]) || @options[:spoiler_text].present?
    @text         = @options.delete(:spoiler_text) if @text.blank? && @options[:spoiler_text].present? && @quoted_status.blank?
    @visibility   = @options[:visibility] || @account.user&.setting_default_privacy
    @visibility   = :unlisted if @visibility&.to_sym == :public && @account.silenced?
    @visibility   = :private if @quoted_status&.private_visibility? && %i(public unlisted).include?(@visibility&.to_sym)
    @scheduled_at = @options[:scheduled_at]&.to_datetime
    @scheduled_at = nil if scheduled_in_the_past?
  rescue ArgumentError
    raise ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
  end

  def process_status!
    @status = @account.statuses.new(status_attributes)
    process_mentions_service.call(@status)
    safeguard_mentions!(@status)
    safeguard_private_mention_quote!(@status)
    attach_tagged_objects!(@status)
    attach_quote!(@status)

    antispam = Antispam.new(@status)
    antispam.local_preflight_check!

    # The following transaction block is needed to wrap the UPDATEs to
    # the media attachments when the status is created
    ApplicationRecord.transaction do
      @status.save!
    end
  end

  def safeguard_private_mention_quote!(status)
    return if @quoted_status.nil? || @visibility.to_sym != :direct

    # The mentions array test here is awkward because the relationship is not persisted at this time
    return if @quoted_status.account_id == @account.id || status.mentions.to_a.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == @quoted_status.account_id && !mention.silent }

    status.errors.add(:base, I18n.t('statuses.errors.quoted_user_not_mentioned'))
    raise ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid, status
  end

  def attach_quote!(status)
    return if @quoted_status.nil?

    status.quote = Quote.create(quoted_status: @quoted_status, status: status)
    status.quote.ensure_quoted_access

    status.quote.accept! if @quoted_status.local? && StatusPolicy.new(@status.account, @quoted_status).quote?
  end

  def attach_tagged_objects!(status)
    ProcessLinksService.new.call(status)
  end

  def safeguard_mentions!(status)
    return if @options[:allowed_mentions].nil?

    expected_account_ids = @options[:allowed_mentions].map(&:to_i)

    unexpected_accounts = status.mentions.map(&:account).to_a.reject { |mentioned_account| expected_account_ids.include?(mentioned_account.id) }
    return if unexpected_accounts.empty?

    raise UnexpectedMentionsError.new('Post would be sent to unexpected accounts', unexpected_accounts)
  end

  def schedule_status!
    status_for_validation = @account.statuses.build(status_attributes)
    safeguard_private_mention_quote!(status_for_validation)

    antispam = Antispam.new(status_for_validation)
    antispam.local_preflight_check!

    if status_for_validation.valid?
      # Marking the status as destroyed is necessary to prevent the status from being
      # persisted when the associated media attachments get updated when creating the
      # scheduled status.
      status_for_validation.destroy

      # The following transaction block is needed to wrap the UPDATEs to
      # the media attachments when the scheduled status is created

      ApplicationRecord.transaction do
        @status = @account.scheduled_statuses.create!(scheduled_status_attributes)
      end
    else
      raise ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid
    end
  rescue Antispam::SilentlyDrop
    @status = @account.scheduled_status.new(scheduled_status_attributes).tap(&:delete)
  end

  def postprocess_status!
    process_hashtags_service.call(@status)
    Trends.tags.register(@status)
    LinkCrawlWorker.perform_async(@status.id)
    DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id)
    process_email_subscriptions!
    ActivityPub::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id)
    PollExpirationNotifyWorker.perform_at(@status.poll.expires_at, @status.poll.id) if @status.poll
    ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker.perform_async(@status.quote.id) if @status.quote&.quoted_status.present? && !@status.quote&.quoted_status&.local?
  end

  def process_email_subscriptions!
    return unless Rails.application.config.x.email_subscriptions && Setting.email_subscriptions &&
                  @status.public_visibility? && (!@status.reply? || @status.in_reply_to_account_id == @status.account_id) &&
                  @status.account.user_can?(:manage_email_subscriptions) &&
                  @status.account.user_email_subscriptions_enabled?

    # To allow e-mail grouping, pass the arguments via a redis set and schedule
    # a unique worker a few minutes in the future, in case the user makes subsequent
    # posts within that time window
    redis.sadd("email_subscriptions:#{@status.account_id}:next_batch", @status.id)
    redis.expire("email_subscriptions:#{@status.account_id}:next_batch", EMAIL_DISTRIBUTION_TTL)
    EmailDistributionWorker.perform_in(EMAIL_DISTRIBUTION_DELAY, @status.account_id)
  end

  def validate_media!
    if @options[:media_ids].blank? || !@options[:media_ids].is_a?(Enumerable)
      @media = []
      return
    end

    raise Mastodon::ValidationError, I18n.t('media_attachments.validations.too_many') if @options[:media_ids].size > Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT

    @media = @account.media_attachments.where(status_id: nil).where(id: @options[:media_ids].take(Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT).map(&:to_i))

    not_found_ids = @options[:media_ids].map(&:to_i) - @media.map(&:id)
    raise Mastodon::ValidationError, I18n.t('media_attachments.validations.not_found', ids: not_found_ids.join(', ')) if not_found_ids.any?

    raise Mastodon::ValidationError, I18n.t('media_attachments.validations.images_and_video') if @media.size > 1 && @media.find(&:audio_or_video?)
    raise Mastodon::ValidationError, I18n.t('media_attachments.validations.not_ready') if @media.any?(&:not_processed?)
  end

  def process_mentions_service
    ProcessMentionsService.new
  end

  def process_hashtags_service
    ProcessHashtagsService.new
  end

  def scheduled?
    @scheduled_at.present?
  end

  def idempotency_key
    "idempotency:status:#{@account.id}:#{@options[:idempotency]}"
  end

  def idempotency_given?
    @options[:idempotency].present?
  end

  def idempotency_duplicate
    if scheduled?
      @account.scheduled_statuses.find(@idempotency_duplicate)
    else
      @account.statuses.find(@idempotency_duplicate)
    end
  end

  def idempotency_duplicate?
    @idempotency_duplicate = redis.get(idempotency_key)
  end

  def with_idempotency
    return yield unless idempotency_given?

    with_redis_lock("idempotency:lock:status:#{@account.id}:#{@options[:idempotency]}") do
      return idempotency_duplicate if idempotency_duplicate?

      yield

      redis.setex(idempotency_key, 3_600, @status.id)
    end
  end

  def scheduled_in_the_past?
    @scheduled_at.present? && @scheduled_at <= Time.now.utc
  end

  def bump_potential_friendship!
    return if !@status.reply? || @account.id == @status.in_reply_to_account_id

    ActivityTracker.increment('activity:interactions')
  end

  def status_attributes
    {
      text: @text,
      media_attachments: @media || [],
      ordered_media_attachment_ids: (@options[:media_ids] || []).map(&:to_i) & @media.map(&:id),
      thread: @in_reply_to,
      poll_attributes: poll_attributes,
      sensitive: @sensitive,
      spoiler_text: @options[:spoiler_text] || '',
      visibility: @visibility,
      language: valid_locale_cascade(@options[:language], @account.user&.preferred_posting_language, I18n.default_locale),
      application: @options[:application],
      rate_limit: @options[:with_rate_limit],
      quote_approval_policy: @options[:quote_approval_policy],
    }.compact
  end

  def scheduled_status_attributes
    {
      scheduled_at: @scheduled_at,
      media_attachments: @media || [],
      params: scheduled_options,
    }
  end

  def poll_attributes
    return if @options[:poll].blank?

    @options[:poll].merge(account: @account, voters_count: 0)
  end

  def scheduled_options
    @options.dup.tap do |options_hash|
      options_hash[:in_reply_to_id]  = options_hash.delete(:thread)&.id
      options_hash[:application_id]  = options_hash.delete(:application)&.id
      options_hash[:quoted_status_id] = options_hash.delete(:quoted_status)&.id
      options_hash[:scheduled_at]    = nil
      options_hash[:idempotency]     = nil
      options_hash[:with_rate_limit] = false
    end
  end
end

=== fan_out_on_write_service.rb ===
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class FanOutOnWriteService < BaseService
     4	  include Redisable
     5	
     6	  # Push a status into home and mentions feeds
     7	  # @param [Status] status
     8	  # @param [Hash] options
     9	  # @option options [Boolean] update
    10	  # @option options [Array<Integer>] silenced_account_ids
    11	  # @option options [Boolean] skip_notifications
    12	  def call(status, options = {})
    13	    @status    = status
    14	    @account   = status.account
    15	    @options   = options
    16	
    17	    return if @status.proper.account.suspended?
    18	
    19	    check_race_condition!
    20	    warm_payload_cache!
    21	
    22	    fan_out_to_local_recipients!
    23	    fan_out_to_public_recipients! if broadcastable?
    24	    fan_out_to_public_streams! if broadcastable?
    25	  end
    26	
    27	  private
    28	
    29	  def check_race_condition!
    30	    # I don't know why but at some point we had an issue where
    31	    # this service was being executed with status objects
    32	    # that had a null visibility - which should not be possible
    33	    # since the column in the database is not nullable.
    34	    #
    35	    # This check re-queues the service to be run at a later time
    36	    # with the full object, if something like it occurs
    37	
    38	    raise Mastodon::RaceConditionError if @status.visibility.nil?
    39	  end
    40	
    41	  def fan_out_to_local_recipients!
    42	    deliver_to_self!
    43	
    44	    unless @options[:skip_notifications]
    45	      notify_quoted_account!
    46	      notify_mentioned_accounts!
    47	      notify_about_update! if update?
    48	    end
    49	
    50	    case @status.visibility.to_sym
    51	    when :public, :unlisted, :private
    52	      deliver_to_all_followers!
    53	      deliver_to_lists!
    54	    when :limited
    55	      deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
    56	    else
    57	      deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
    58	      deliver_to_conversation!
    59	    end
    60	  end
    61	
    62	  def fan_out_to_public_recipients!
    63	    deliver_to_hashtag_followers!
    64	  end
    65	
    66	  def fan_out_to_public_streams!
    67	    broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!
    68	    broadcast_to_public_streams!
    69	  end
    70	
    71	  def deliver_to_self!
    72	    FeedManager.instance.push_to_home(@account, @status, update: update?) if @account.local?
    73	  end
    74	
    75	  def notify_quoted_account!
    76	    return unless @status.quote&.quoted_account&.local? && @status.quote&.accepted?
    77	
    78	    LocalNotificationWorker.perform_async(@status.quote.quoted_account_id, @status.quote.id, 'Quote', 'quote')
    79	  end
    80	
    81	  def notify_mentioned_accounts!
    82	    @status.active_mentions.joins(:account).merge(Account.local).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |mentions|
    83	      LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(mentions) do |mention|
    84	        options = { 'silenced' => true } if @options[:silenced_account_ids]&.include?(mention.account_id)
    85	
    86	        [mention.account_id, mention.id, 'Mention', 'mention', options].compact
    87	      end
    88	
    89	      next unless update?
    90	
    91	      # This may result in duplicate update payloads, but this ensures clients
    92	      # are aware of edits to posts only appearing in mention notifications
    93	      # (e.g. private mentions or mentions by people they do not follow)
    94	      PushUpdateWorker.push_bulk(mentions.filter { |mention| subscribed_to_streaming_api?(mention.account_id) }) do |mention|
    95	        [mention.account_id, @status.id, "timeline:#{mention.account_id}:notifications", { 'update' => true }]
    96	      end
    97	    end
    98	  end
    99	
   100	  def notify_about_update!
   101	    @status.reblogged_by_accounts.merge(Account.local).select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |accounts|
   102	      LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(accounts) do |account|
   103	        [account.id, @status.id, 'Status', 'update']
   104	      end
   105	    end
   106	
   107	    @status.quotes.accepted.find_in_batches do |quotes|
   108	      LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(quotes) do |quote|
   109	        [quote.account_id, quote.status_id, 'Status', 'quoted_update']
   110	      end
   111	    end
   112	  end
   113	
   114	  def deliver_to_all_followers!
   115	    @account.followers_for_local_distribution.select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |followers|
   116	      FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(followers) do |follower|
   117	        [@status.id, follower.id, 'home', { 'update' => update? }]
   118	      end
   119	    end
   120	  end
   121	
   122	  def deliver_to_hashtag_followers!
   123	    TagFollow.for_local_distribution.where(tag_id: @status.tags.map(&:id)).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |follows|
   124	      FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(follows) do |follow|
   125	        [@status.id, follow.account_id, 'tags', { 'update' => update? }]
   126	      end
   127	    end
   128	  end
   129	
   130	  def deliver_to_lists!
   131	    @account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |lists|
   132	      FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(lists) do |list|
   133	        [@status.id, list.id, 'list', { 'update' => update? }]
   134	      end
   135	    end
   136	  end
   137	
   138	  def deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
   139	    @status.mentions.joins(:account).merge(@account.followers_for_local_distribution).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |mentions|
   140	      FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(mentions) do |mention|
   141	        [@status.id, mention.account_id, 'home', { 'update' => update? }]
   142	      end
   143	    end
   144	  end
   145	
   146	  def broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!
   147	    @status.tags.map(&:name).each do |hashtag|
   148	      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}", anonymous_payload)
   149	      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}:local", anonymous_payload) if @status.local?
   150	    end
   151	  end
   152	
   153	  def broadcast_to_public_streams!
   154	    return if @status.reply? && @status.in_reply_to_account_id != @account.id
   155	
   156	    redis.publish('timeline:public', anonymous_payload)
   157	    redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', anonymous_payload)
   158	
   159	    if @status.with_media?
   160	      redis.publish('timeline:public:media', anonymous_payload)
   161	      redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', anonymous_payload)
   162	    end
   163	  end
   164	
   165	  def deliver_to_conversation!
   166	    AccountConversation.add_status(@account, @status) unless update?
   167	  end
   168	
   169	  def warm_payload_cache!
   170	    Rails.cache.write("fan-out/#{@status.id}", rendered_status)
   171	  end
   172	
   173	  def anonymous_payload
   174	    @anonymous_payload ||= JSON.generate({
   175	      event: update? ? :'status.update' : :update,
   176	      payload: rendered_status,
   177	    }.as_json)
   178	  end
   179	
   180	  def rendered_status
   181	    @rendered_status ||= InlineRenderer.render(@status, nil, :status)
   182	  end
   183	
   184	  def update?
   185	    @options[:update]
   186	  end
   187	
   188	  def broadcastable?
   189	    @status.public_visibility? && !@status.reblog? && !@account.silenced?
   190	  end
   191	
   192	  def subscribed_to_streaming_api?(account_id)
   193	    redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{account_id}") || redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{account_id}:notifications")
   194	  end
   195	end

    39	    { 'synchronize_followers' => @status.private_visibility? && @account.followers_count < MAX_FOLLOWERS_FOR_SYNCHRONIZATION }
    40	  end
    41	end
=== fan_out_on_write_service.rb ===
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class FanOutOnWriteService < BaseService
     4	  include Redisable
     5	
     6	  # Push a status into home and mentions feeds
     7	  # @param [Status] status
     8	  # @param [Hash] options
     9	  # @option options [Boolean] update
    10	  # @option options [Array<Integer>] silenced_account_ids
    11	  # @option options [Boolean] skip_notifications
    12	  def call(status, options = {})
    13	    @status    = status
    14	    @account   = status.account
    15	    @options   = options
    16	
    17	    return if @status.proper.account.suspended?
    18	
    19	    check_race_condition!
    20	    warm_payload_cache!
    21	
    22	    fan_out_to_local_recipients!
    23	    fan_out_to_public_recipients! if broadcastable?
    24	    fan_out_to_public_streams! if broadcastable?
    25	  end
    26	
    27	  private
    28	
    29	  def check_race_condition!
    30	    # I don't know why but at some point we had an issue where
    31	    # this service was being executed with status objects
    32	    # that had a null visibility - which should not be possible
    33	    # since the column in the database is not nullable.
    34	    #
    35	    # This check re-queues the service to be run at a later time
    36	    # with the full object, if something like it occurs
    37	
    38	    raise Mastodon::RaceConditionError if @status.visibility.nil?
    39	  end
    40	
    41	  def fan_out_to_local_recipients!
    42	    deliver_to_self!
    43	
    44	    unless @options[:skip_notifications]
    45	      notify_quoted_account!
    46	      notify_mentioned_accounts!
    47	      notify_about_update! if update?
    48	    end
    49	
    50	    case @status.visibility.to_sym
    51	    when :public, :unlisted, :private
    52	      deliver_to_all_followers!
    53	      deliver_to_lists!
    54	    when :limited
    55	      deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
    56	    else
    57	      deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
    58	      deliver_to_conversation!
    59	    end
    60	  end
    61	
    62	  def fan_out_to_public_recipients!
    63	    deliver_to_hashtag_followers!
    64	  end
    65	
    66	  def fan_out_to_public_streams!
    67	    broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!
    68	    broadcast_to_public_streams!
    69	  end
    70	
    71	  def deliver_to_self!
    72	    FeedManager.instance.push_to_home(@account, @status, update: update?) if @account.local?
    73	  end
    74	
    75	  def notify_quoted_account!
    76	    return unless @status.quote&.quoted_account&.local? && @status.quote&.accepted?
    77	
    78	    LocalNotificationWorker.perform_async(@status.quote.quoted_account_id, @status.quote.id, 'Quote', 'quote')
    79	  end
    80	
    81	  def notify_mentioned_accounts!
    82	    @status.active_mentions.joins(:account).merge(Account.local).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |mentions|
    83	      LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(mentions) do |mention|
    84	        options = { 'silenced' => true } if @options[:silenced_account_ids]&.include?(mention.account_id)
    85	
    86	        [mention.account_id, mention.id, 'Mention', 'mention', options].compact
    87	      end
    88	
    89	      next unless update?
    90	
    91	      # This may result in duplicate update payloads, but this ensures clients
    92	      # are aware of edits to posts only appearing in mention notifications
    93	      # (e.g. private mentions or mentions by people they do not follow)
    94	      PushUpdateWorker.push_bulk(mentions.filter { |mention| subscribed_to_streaming_api?(mention.account_id) }) do |mention|
    95	        [mention.account_id, @status.id, "timeline:#{mention.account_id}:notifications", { 'update' => true }]
    96	      end
    97	    end
    98	  end
    99	
   100	  def notify_about_update!
   101	    @status.reblogged_by_accounts.merge(Account.local).select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |accounts|
   102	      LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(accounts) do |account|
   103	        [account.id, @status.id, 'Status', 'update']
   104	      end
   105	    end
   106	
   107	    @status.quotes.accepted.find_in_batches do |quotes|
   108	      LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk(quotes) do |quote|
   109	        [quote.account_id, quote.status_id, 'Status', 'quoted_update']
   110	      end
   111	    end
   112	  end
   113	
   114	  def deliver_to_all_followers!
   115	    @account.followers_for_local_distribution.select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |followers|
   116	      FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(followers) do |follower|
   117	        [@status.id, follower.id, 'home', { 'update' => update? }]
   118	      end
   119	    end
   120	  end
   121	
   122	  def deliver_to_hashtag_followers!
   123	    TagFollow.for_local_distribution.where(tag_id: @status.tags.map(&:id)).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |follows|
   124	      FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(follows) do |follow|
   125	        [@status.id, follow.account_id, 'tags', { 'update' => update? }]
   126	      end
   127	    end
   128	  end
   129	
   130	  def deliver_to_lists!
   131	    @account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |lists|
   132	      FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(lists) do |list|
   133	        [@status.id, list.id, 'list', { 'update' => update? }]
   134	      end
   135	    end
   136	  end
   137	
   138	  def deliver_to_mentioned_followers!
   139	    @status.mentions.joins(:account).merge(@account.followers_for_local_distribution).select(:id, :account_id).reorder(nil).find_in_batches do |mentions|
   140	      FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk(mentions) do |mention|
   141	        [@status.id, mention.account_id, 'home', { 'update' => update? }]
   142	      end
   143	    end
   144	  end
   145	
   146	  def broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!
   147	    @status.tags.map(&:name).each do |hashtag|
   148	      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}", anonymous_payload)
   149	      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}:local", anonymous_payload) if @status.local?
   150	    end
   151	  end
   152	
   153	  def broadcast_to_public_streams!
   154	    return if @status.reply? && @status.in_reply_to_account_id != @account.id
   155	
   156	    redis.publish('timeline:public', anonymous_payload)
   157	    redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', anonymous_payload)
   158	
   159	    if @status.with_media?
   160	      redis.publish('timeline:public:media', anonymous_payload)
   161	      redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', anonymous_payload)
   162	    end
   163	  end
   164	
   165	  def deliver_to_conversation!
   166	    AccountConversation.add_status(@account, @status) unless update?
   167	  end
   168	
   169	  def warm_payload_cache!
   170	    Rails.cache.write("fan-out/#{@status.id}", rendered_status)
   171	  end
   172	
   173	  def anonymous_payload
   174	    @anonymous_payload ||= JSON.generate({
   175	      event: update? ? :'status.update' : :update,
   176	      payload: rendered_status,
   177	    }.as_json)
   178	  end
   179	
   180	  def rendered_status
   181	    @rendered_status ||= InlineRenderer.render(@status, nil, :status)
   182	  end
   183	
   184	  def update?
   185	    @options[:update]
   186	  end
   187	
   188	  def broadcastable?
   189	    @status.public_visibility? && !@status.reblog? && !@account.silenced?
   190	  end
   191	
   192	  def subscribed_to_streaming_api?(account_id)
   193	    redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{account_id}") || redis.exists?("subscribed:timeline:#{account_id}:notifications")
   194	  end
   195	end

=== remove_status_service.rb ===
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class RemoveStatusService < BaseService
     4	  include Redisable
     5	  include Payloadable
     6	  include Lockable
     7	
     8	  # Delete a status
     9	  # @param   [Status] status
    10	  # @param   [Hash] options
    11	  # @option  [Boolean] :redraft
    12	  # @option  [Boolean] :immediate
    13	  # @option  [Boolean] :preserve
    14	  # @option  [Boolean] :original_removed
    15	  # @option  [Boolean] :skip_streaming
    16	  def call(status, **options)
    17	    @payload  = { event: :delete, payload: status.id.to_s }.to_json
    18	    @status   = status
    19	    @account  = status.account
    20	    @options  = options
    21	
    22	    with_redis_lock("distribute:#{@status.id}") do
    23	      @status.discard_with_reblogs
    24	
    25	      StatusPin.find_by(status: @status)&.destroy
    26	
    27	      remove_from_self if @account.local?
    28	      remove_from_followers
    29	      remove_from_lists
    30	
    31	      # There is no reason to send out Undo activities when the
    32	      # cause is that the original object has been removed, since
    33	      # original object being removed implicitly removes reblogs
    34	      # of it. The Delete activity of the original is forwarded
    35	      # separately.
    36	      remove_from_remote_reach if @account.local? && !@options[:original_removed]
    37	
    38	      # Since reblogs don't mention anyone, don't get reblogged,
    39	      # favourited and don't contain their own media attachments
    40	      # or hashtags, this can be skipped
    41	      unless @status.reblog?
    42	        remove_from_mentions
    43	        remove_reblogs
    44	        remove_from_hashtags
    45	        remove_from_public
    46	        remove_from_media if @status.with_media?
    47	        remove_media
    48	      end
    49	
    50	      # Revoke the quote while we get a chance… maybe this should be a `before_destroy` hook?
    51	      RevokeQuoteService.new.call(@status.quote) if @status.quote&.quoted_account&.local? && @status.quote&.accepted?
    52	
    53	      @status.destroy! if permanently?
    54	    end
    55	  end
    56	
    57	  private
    58	
    59	  # The following FeedManager calls all do not result in redis publishes for
    60	  # streaming, as the `:update` option is false
    61	
    62	  def remove_from_self
    63	    FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(@account, @status)
    64	  end
    65	
    66	  def remove_from_followers
    67	    @account.followers_for_local_distribution.includes(:user).reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
    68	      FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(follower, @status)
    69	    end
    70	  end
    71	
    72	  def remove_from_lists
    73	    @account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id, :account_id).includes(account: :user).reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
    74	      FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_list(list, @status)
    75	    end
    76	  end
    77	
    78	  def remove_from_mentions
    79	    # For limited visibility statuses, the mentions that determine
    80	    # who receives them in their home feed are a subset of followers
    81	    # and therefore the delete is already handled by sending it to all
    82	    # followers. Here we send a delete to actively mentioned accounts
    83	    # that may not follow the account
    84	
    85	    return if skip_streaming?
    86	
    87	    @status.active_mentions.find_each do |mention|
    88	      redis.publish("timeline:#{mention.account_id}", @payload)
    89	    end
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def remove_from_remote_reach
    93	    # Followers, relays, people who got mentioned in the status,
    94	    # or who reblogged it from someone else might not follow
    95	    # the author and wouldn't normally receive the delete
    96	    # notification - so here, we explicitly send it to them
    97	
    98	    status_reach_finder = StatusReachFinder.new(@status, unsafe: true)
    99	
   100	    ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker.push_bulk(status_reach_finder.inboxes, limit: 1_000) do |inbox_url|
   101	      [signed_activity_json, @account.id, inbox_url]
   102	    end
   103	  end
   104	
   105	  def signed_activity_json
   106	    @signed_activity_json ||= serialize_payload(@status, @status.reblog? ? ActivityPub::UndoAnnounceSerializer : ActivityPub::DeleteNoteSerializer, signer: @account, always_sign: true).to_json
   107	  end
   108	
   109	  def remove_reblogs
   110	    # We delete reblogs of the status before the original status,
   111	    # because once original status is gone, reblogs will disappear
   112	    # without us being able to do all the fancy stuff
   113	
   114	    @status.reblogs.rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at]).includes(:account).reorder(nil).find_each do |reblog|
   115	      RemoveStatusService.new.call(reblog, original_removed: true, skip_streaming: skip_streaming?)
   116	    end
   117	  end
   118	
   119	  def remove_from_hashtags
   120	    @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: @status.tags.map(&:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
   121	      featured_tag.decrement(@status)
   122	    end
   123	
   124	    return unless @status.public_visibility?
   125	
   126	    return if skip_streaming?
   127	
   128	    @status.tags.map(&:name).each do |hashtag|
   129	      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}", @payload)
   130	      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}:local", @payload) if @status.local?
   131	    end
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  def remove_from_public
   135	    return unless @status.public_visibility?
   136	
   137	    return if skip_streaming?
   138	
   139	    redis.publish('timeline:public', @payload)
   140	    redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', @payload)
   141	  end
   142	
   143	  def remove_from_media
   144	    return unless @status.public_visibility?
   145	
   146	    return if skip_streaming?
   147	
   148	    redis.publish('timeline:public:media', @payload)
   149	    redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', @payload)
   150	  end
   151	
   152	  def remove_media
   153	    return if @options[:redraft]
   154	
   155	    if permanently?
   156	      @status.media_attachments.destroy_all
   157	    else
   158	      UpdateMediaAttachmentsPermissionsService.new.call(@status.media_attachments, :private)
   159	    end
   160	  end
   161	
   162	  def permanently?
   163	    @options[:immediate] || !(@options[:preserve] || @status.reported?)
   164	  end
   165	
   166	  def skip_streaming?
   167	    !!@options[:skip_streaming]
   168	  end
   169	end
=== batched_remove_status_service.rb ===
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class BatchedRemoveStatusService < BaseService
     4	  include Redisable
     5	
     6	  # Delete multiple statuses and reblogs of them as efficiently as possible
     7	  # @param [Enumerable<Status>] statuses An array of statuses
     8	  # @param [Hash] options
     9	  # @option [Boolean] :skip_side_effects Do not modify feeds and send updates to streaming API
    10	  def call(statuses, **options)
    11	    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
    12	      records: statuses,
    13	      associations: options[:skip_side_effects] ? :reblogs : [:account, :tags, reblogs: :account]
    14	    ).call
    15	
    16	    statuses_and_reblogs = statuses.flat_map { |status| [status] + status.reblogs }
    17	
    18	    # The conversations for direct visibility statuses also need
    19	    # to be manually updated. This part is not efficient but we
    20	    # rely on direct visibility statuses being relatively rare.
    21	    statuses_with_account_conversations = statuses.select(&:direct_visibility?)
    22	
    23	    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
    24	      records: statuses_with_account_conversations,
    25	      associations: [mentions: :account]
    26	    ).call
    27	
    28	    statuses_with_account_conversations.each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
    29	
    30	    # We do not batch all deletes into one to avoid having a long-running
    31	    # transaction lock the database, but we use the delete method instead
    32	    # of destroy to avoid all callbacks. We rely on foreign keys to
    33	    # cascade the delete faster without loading the associations.
    34	    statuses_and_reblogs.each_slice(50) { |slice| Status.unscoped.where(id: slice.pluck(:id)).delete_all }
    35	
    36	    # Since we skipped all callbacks, we also need to manually
    37	    # deindex the statuses
    38	    if Chewy.enabled?
    39	      Chewy.strategy.current.update(StatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
    40	      Chewy.strategy.current.update(PublicStatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
    41	    end
    42	
    43	    return if options[:skip_side_effects]
    44	
    45	    # Batch by source account
    46	    statuses_and_reblogs.group_by(&:account_id).each_value do |account_statuses|
    47	      account = account_statuses.first.account
    48	
    49	      next unless account
    50	
    51	      unpush_from_home_timelines(account, account_statuses)
    52	      unpush_from_list_timelines(account, account_statuses)
    53	    end
    54	
    55	    # Cannot be batched
    56	    @status_id_cutoff = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(2.weeks.ago)
    57	    redis.pipelined do |pipeline|
    58	      statuses.each do |status|
    59	        unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
    60	      end
    61	    end
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  private
    65	
    66	  def unpush_from_home_timelines(account, statuses)
    67	    account.followers_for_local_distribution.includes(:user).reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
    68	      statuses.each do |status|
    69	        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(follower, status)
    70	      end
    71	    end
    72	  end
    73	
    74	  def unpush_from_list_timelines(account, statuses)
    75	    account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id, :account_id).includes(account: :user).reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
    76	      statuses.each do |status|
    77	        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_list(list, status)
    78	      end
    79	    end
    80	  end
    81	
    82	  def unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
    83	    return unless status.public_visibility? && status.id > @status_id_cutoff
    84	
    85	    payload = { event: :delete, payload: status.id.to_s }.to_json
    86	
    87	    pipeline.publish('timeline:public', payload)
    88	    pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', payload)
    89	
    90	    if status.media_attachments.any?
    91	      pipeline.publish('timeline:public:media', payload)
    92	      pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', payload)
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    status.tags.map { |tag| tag.name.downcase }.each do |hashtag|
    96	      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}", payload)
    97	      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}:local", payload) if status.local?
    98	    end
    99	  end
   100	end

    83	    # that may not follow the account
    84	
    85	    return if skip_streaming?
    86	
    87	    @status.active_mentions.find_each do |mention|
    88	      redis.publish("timeline:#{mention.account_id}", @payload)
    89	    end
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  def remove_from_remote_reach
    93	    # Followers, relays, people who got mentioned in the status,
    94	    # or who reblogged it from someone else might not follow
    95	    # the author and wouldn't normally receive the delete
    96	    # notification - so here, we explicitly send it to them
    97	
    98	    status_reach_finder = StatusReachFinder.new(@status, unsafe: true)
    99	
   100	    ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker.push_bulk(status_reach_finder.inboxes, limit: 1_000) do |inbox_url|
   101	      [signed_activity_json, @account.id, inbox_url]
   102	    end
   103	  end
   104	
   105	  def signed_activity_json
   106	    @signed_activity_json ||= serialize_payload(@status, @status.reblog? ? ActivityPub::UndoAnnounceSerializer : ActivityPub::DeleteNoteSerializer, signer: @account, always_sign: true).to_json
   107	  end
   108	
   109	  def remove_reblogs
   110	    # We delete reblogs of the status before the original status,
   111	    # because once original status is gone, reblogs will disappear
   112	    # without us being able to do all the fancy stuff
   113	
   114	    @status.reblogs.rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at]).includes(:account).reorder(nil).find_each do |reblog|
   115	      RemoveStatusService.new.call(reblog, original_removed: true, skip_streaming: skip_streaming?)
   116	    end
   117	  end
   118	
   119	  def remove_from_hashtags
   120	    @account.featured_tags.where(tag_id: @status.tags.map(&:id)).find_each do |featured_tag|
   121	      featured_tag.decrement(@status)
   122	    end
   123	
   124	    return unless @status.public_visibility?
   125	
   126	    return if skip_streaming?
   127	
   128	    @status.tags.map(&:name).each do |hashtag|
   129	      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}", @payload)
   130	      redis.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag.downcase}:local", @payload) if @status.local?
   131	    end
   132	  end
   133	
   134	  def remove_from_public
   135	    return unless @status.public_visibility?
   136	
   137	    return if skip_streaming?
   138	
   139	    redis.publish('timeline:public', @payload)
   140	    redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', @payload)
   141	  end
   142	
   143	  def remove_from_media
   144	    return unless @status.public_visibility?
   145	
   146	    return if skip_streaming?
   147	
   148	    redis.publish('timeline:public:media', @payload)
   149	    redis.publish(@status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', @payload)
   150	  end
   151	
   152	  def remove_media
   153	    return if @options[:redraft]
   154	
   155	    if permanently?
   156	      @status.media_attachments.destroy_all
   157	    else
   158	      UpdateMediaAttachmentsPermissionsService.new.call(@status.media_attachments, :private)
   159	    end
   160	  end
   161	
   162	  def permanently?
   163	    @options[:immediate] || !(@options[:preserve] || @status.reported?)
   164	  end
   165	
   166	  def skip_streaming?
   167	    !!@options[:skip_streaming]
   168	  end
   169	end
=== batched_remove_status_service.rb ===
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class BatchedRemoveStatusService < BaseService
     4	  include Redisable
     5	
     6	  # Delete multiple statuses and reblogs of them as efficiently as possible
     7	  # @param [Enumerable<Status>] statuses An array of statuses
     8	  # @param [Hash] options
     9	  # @option [Boolean] :skip_side_effects Do not modify feeds and send updates to streaming API
    10	  def call(statuses, **options)
    11	    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
    12	      records: statuses,
    13	      associations: options[:skip_side_effects] ? :reblogs : [:account, :tags, reblogs: :account]
    14	    ).call
    15	
    16	    statuses_and_reblogs = statuses.flat_map { |status| [status] + status.reblogs }
    17	
    18	    # The conversations for direct visibility statuses also need
    19	    # to be manually updated. This part is not efficient but we
    20	    # rely on direct visibility statuses being relatively rare.
    21	    statuses_with_account_conversations = statuses.select(&:direct_visibility?)
    22	
    23	    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
    24	      records: statuses_with_account_conversations,
    25	      associations: [mentions: :account]
    26	    ).call
    27	
    28	    statuses_with_account_conversations.each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
    29	
    30	    # We do not batch all deletes into one to avoid having a long-running
    31	    # transaction lock the database, but we use the delete method instead
    32	    # of destroy to avoid all callbacks. We rely on foreign keys to
    33	    # cascade the delete faster without loading the associations.
    34	    statuses_and_reblogs.each_slice(50) { |slice| Status.unscoped.where(id: slice.pluck(:id)).delete_all }
    35	
    36	    # Since we skipped all callbacks, we also need to manually
    37	    # deindex the statuses
    38	    if Chewy.enabled?
    39	      Chewy.strategy.current.update(StatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
    40	      Chewy.strategy.current.update(PublicStatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
    41	    end
    42	
    43	    return if options[:skip_side_effects]
    44	
    45	    # Batch by source account
    46	    statuses_and_reblogs.group_by(&:account_id).each_value do |account_statuses|
    47	      account = account_statuses.first.account
    48	
    49	      next unless account
    50	
    51	      unpush_from_home_timelines(account, account_statuses)
    52	      unpush_from_list_timelines(account, account_statuses)
    53	    end
    54	
    55	    # Cannot be batched
    56	    @status_id_cutoff = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(2.weeks.ago)
    57	    redis.pipelined do |pipeline|
    58	      statuses.each do |status|
    59	        unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
    60	      end
    61	    end
    62	  end
    63	
    64	  private
    65	
    66	  def unpush_from_home_timelines(account, statuses)
    67	    account.followers_for_local_distribution.includes(:user).reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
    68	      statuses.each do |status|
    69	        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(follower, status)
    70	      end
    71	    end
    72	  end
    73	
    74	  def unpush_from_list_timelines(account, statuses)
    75	    account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id, :account_id).includes(account: :user).reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
    76	      statuses.each do |status|
    77	        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_list(list, status)
    78	      end
    79	    end
    80	  end
    81	
    82	  def unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
    83	    return unless status.public_visibility? && status.id > @status_id_cutoff
    84	
    85	    payload = { event: :delete, payload: status.id.to_s }.to_json
    86	
    87	    pipeline.publish('timeline:public', payload)
    88	    pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', payload)
    89	
    90	    if status.media_attachments.any?
    91	      pipeline.publish('timeline:public:media', payload)
    92	      pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', payload)
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    status.tags.map { |tag| tag.name.downcase }.each do |hashtag|
    96	      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}", payload)
    97	      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}:local", payload) if status.local?
    98	    end
    99	  end
   100	end

     4	  attr_reader :status, :account

    12	    return false if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id
    13	
    14	    blocked_by_policy? || (account_present? && filtered_status?) || silenced_account?

    17	  def filter_state_for_quote
    18	    if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id
    19	      nil
    20	    elsif blocked_by_policy?
    21	      'unauthorized'
    22	    elsif account_present? && blocking_domain?
    23	      'blocked_domain'
    24	    elsif account_present? && blocking_account?
    25	      'blocked_account'
    26	    elsif account_present? && muting_account?

    36

    44

     5	  # @param [Hash] options
     6	  # @option options [Boolean] :unsafe
     7	  def initialize(status, options = {})

    11	
    12	  def inboxes

    23	  def reached_account_ids
    24	    # When the status is a reblog, there are no interactions with it
    25	    # directly, we assume all interactions are with the original one

     4	  attr_reader :status, :account
     5	
     6	  def initialize(status, account)
     7	    @status              = status

     9	  end
    10	
    11	  def filtered?
    12	    return false if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id
    13	
    14	    blocked_by_policy? || (account_present? && filtered_status?) || silenced_account?
    15	  end
    16	
    17	  def filter_state_for_quote
    18	    if !account.nil? && account.id == status.account_id
    19	      nil
    20	    elsif blocked_by_policy?
    21	      'unauthorized'
    22	    elsif account_present? && blocking_domain?
    23	      'blocked_domain'
    24	    elsif account_present? && blocking_account?
    25	      'blocked_account'
    26	    elsif account_present? && muting_account?
    27	      'muted_account'
    28	    end
    29	  end
    30	
    31	  private
    32	
    33	  def account_present?
    34	    !account.nil?
    35	  end
    36	
    37	  def filtered_status?
    38	    blocking_account? || blocking_domain? || muting_account?
    39	  end
    40	
    41	  def blocking_account?
    42	    account.blocking?(status.account_id)
    43	  end
    44	
    45	  def blocking_domain?
    46	    account.domain_blocking?(status.account_domain)
    47	  end
    48	
    49	  def muting_account?
    50	    account.muting?(status.account_id)
    51	  end
    52	
    53	  def silenced_account?
    54	    status_account_silenced? && !account_following_status_account?
    55	  end

    57	  def status_account_silenced?

    59	  end
    60	
    61	  def account_following_status_account?
    62	    account&.following?(status.account_id)
    63	  end
    64	
    65	  def blocked_by_policy?
    66	    !policy_allows_show?
    67	  end
    68	
    69	  def policy_allows_show?
    70	    StatusPolicy.new(account, status).show?
    71	  end
    72	end

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class Vacuum::StatusesVacuum
     4	  include Redisable
     5	
     6	  def initialize(retention_period)
     7	    @retention_period = retention_period
     8	  end
     9	
    10	  def perform
    11	    vacuum_statuses! if @retention_period.present?
    12	  end
    13	
    14	  private
    15	
    16	  def vacuum_statuses!
    17	    statuses_scope.in_batches do |statuses|
    18	      # Side-effects not covered by foreign keys, such
    19	      # as the search index, must be handled first.
    20	      statuses.direct_visibility
    21	        .includes(mentions: :account)
    22	        .find_each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
    23	      if Chewy.enabled?
    24	        remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:StatusesIndex')
    25	        remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:PublicStatusesIndex')
    26	      end
    27	
    28	      # Foreign keys take care of most associated records for us.
    29	      # Media attachments will be orphaned.
    30	      statuses.delete_all
    31	    end
    32	  end
    33	
    34	  def statuses_scope
    35	    Status.unscoped.kept
    36	      .joins(:account).merge(Account.remote)
    37	      .where(statuses: { id: ...retention_period_as_id })
    38	  end

content.text:
I now have a comprehensive picture. Here is the full audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Mastodon — a Ruby on Rails 8 + (Vite/React frontend) ActivityPub-federated social server (`README.md:21`). Standard Rails layout under `app/`: `models/`, `services/` (business-logic command objects, the real workhorses), `workers/` (Sidekiq async jobs), `lib/` (plain service/utility objects), `policies/` (Pundit authorization), `serializers/` (REST + ActivityPub output), `presenters/`, `chewy/` (Elasticsearch indices), plus a Node `streaming/` server for real-time timelines.

**Central content model:** `app/models/status.rb` — `Status` is the post. Everything (feeds, notifications, federation, moderation, search, trends) revolves around it. It is an `ApplicationRecord` using soft-delete (`Discard::Model`, `deleted_at`) with a `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`).

**Shared pieces it is built on:**
- Generic concerns: `app/models/concerns/cacheable.rb`, `paginable.rb`, `rate_limitable.rb`; `Discard::Model` (gem); `Paginable`.
- Status-namespaced concerns in `app/models/concerns/status/`: `visibility.rb`, `threading_concern.rb`, `snapshot_concern.rb`, `search_concern.rb`, `interaction_policy_concern.rb`, `fasp_concern.rb`, `fetch_replies_concern.rb`, `safe_reblog_insert.rb`.
- Satellite models: `StatusStat`, `StatusEdit`, `StatusPin`, `StatusTrend`, `Mention`, `Favourite`, `Bookmark`, `Poll`, `Quote`, `Conversation`, `MediaAttachment`, `PreviewCardsStatus`, `TaggedObject`, `CustomFilterStatus`.
- Service/lib helpers: `app/lib/feed_manager.rb`, `status_filter.rb`, `account_statuses_filter.rb`, `status_reach_finder.rb`, `status_finder.rb`, `status_cache_hydrator.rb`, `app/policies/status_policy.rb`.

---

## Step 2 — The Status contract & what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb:34`.

**Included concerns** (`status.rb:35-46`):
| Concern | file:line | Contributes |
|---|---|---|
| `Cacheable` | `concerns/cacheable.rb:3` | `cache_associated`/`with_includes`/`preload_cacheable_associations` — drives `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS` (`status.rb:48-60,180-184`) |
| `Discard::Model` | gem; `status.rb:36,66` | soft delete via `deleted_at`; `kept`/`discarded` scopes; powers `default_scope` (`status.rb:128`) |
| `Paginable` | `concerns/paginable.rb:3` | `paginate_by_max_id`/`paginate_by_min_id` (all timeline pagination) |
| `RateLimitable` | `concerns/rate_limitable.rb:3` | `rate_limit by: :account` (`status.rb:64`) — post rate limiting |
| `Status::FaspConcern` | `concerns/status/fasp_concern.rb:3` | after_commit announce new/update/delete/trend to FASP providers |
| `Status::FetchRepliesConcern` | `concerns/status/fetch_replies_concern.rb:3` | reply-fetch scopes + `should_fetch_replies?` |
| `Status::SafeReblogInsert` | `concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb:3` | overrides `_insert_record` to block reblogs of discarded statuses at SQL level |
| `Status::SearchConcern` | `concerns/status/search_concern.rb:3` | `indexable` scope, `searchable_by`/`searchable_text`/`searchable_properties` |
| `Status::SnapshotConcern` | `concerns/status/snapshot_concern.rb:3` | `has_many :edits` (StatusEdit, dependent: :destroy); `build_snapshot`/`snapshot!` |
| `Status::ThreadingConcern` | `concerns/status/threading_concern.rb:3` | `ancestors`/`descendants`/`self_replies`/`permitted_statuses_from_ids` (recursive CTE thread walk) |
| `Status::Visibility` | `concerns/status/visibility.rb:3` | `visibility` enum, `distributable?`/`hidden?`, `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` scopes |
| `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern` | `concerns/status/interaction_policy_concern.rb:3` | `quote_interaction_policy` composed_of; `quote_policy_for_account` |

**Associations other records hang off** (`status.rb:75-118`): `account` (`:77`), self-refs `thread`/`reblog`/`replies`/`reblogs` (`:82-93`), `favourites`/`bookmarks`/`mentions` (dependent: :destroy, `:89-94`), `media_attachments` (dependent: :nullify, `:96`), `poll`/`preloadable_poll` (`:80,116`), `quote`/`quotes` (`:98,118`), `conversation`/`owned_conversation` (`:79,87`), `status_stat` (`:115`), `trend`→StatusTrend (`:117`), `preview_cards_status` (dependent: :delete, `:112`), `tags` HABTM (`:110`), `tagged_objects` (`:97`), `notification` polymorphic `as: :activity` (`:114`), `edits` (from SnapshotConcern).

**Teardown contract** (what a teammate MUST know): soft-delete column `deleted_at` (`:66`); `discard_with_reblogs` cascades discard to reblogs (`:400`); `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!, prepend: true` runs before dependent-destroys (`:178,406`); counter caches on create/destroy commit (`:160-161,479-493`); webhooks + URI store + FASP all fire via `after_commit` (`:157-164`); `default_scope` hides discarded rows so most code never sees deleted statuses unless `unscoped`/`with_discarded`.

---

## Step 3 — Creation & distribution flow

| Layer | file:line | Inline / Async |
|---|---|---|
| Entry (local post) | `PostStatusService#call` `app/services/post_status_service.rb:42` | inline (controller) |
| Mentions/links/quote/tags parse | `process_status!` `post_status_service.rb:84-100`; `ProcessMentionsService`, `ProcessLinksService` | inline |
| Persistence | `@status.save!` in txn `post_status_service.rb:97-99` → model callbacks (`status.rb:157-174`: conversation, snowflake id, counter caches, store_uri, webhooks) | inline |
| Post-process fan-out trigger | `postprocess_status!` `post_status_service.rb:162-171` | enqueues async |
| → Local fan-out | `DistributionWorker` `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:8` → `FanOutOnWriteService#call` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` | **async** |
| → home/list/tag feed inserts | `FanOutOnWriteService` `:114-144` → `FeedInsertWorker` → `FeedManager.push_to_home/list` `app/lib/feed_manager.rb:76,102` | async |
| → local notifications (mention/quote/update) | `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:75-112` → `LocalNotificationWorker` | async |
| → public/hashtag streaming | `broadcast_to_public_streams!`/`broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!` `:146-163` (redis publish) | async (inside worker) |
| → federation out | `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker` `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:11` → `StatusReachFinder#inboxes` `:23` → `ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker` | **async** |
| Link preview | `LinkCrawlWorker` `post_status_service.rb:165` | async |
| Email digests | `process_email_subscriptions!` `:173` → `EmailDistributionWorker` | async |
| Poll expiry / quote request | `:169-170` → `PollExpirationNotifyWorker`, `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker` | async |
| Reblog path | `ReblogService` `app/services/reblog_service.rb` (mirrors: builds Status, DistributionWorker + ActivityPub::DistributionWorker) | inline→async |
| Edit path | `UpdateStatusService` `app/services/update_status_service.rb` + federated `ActivityPub::ProcessStatusUpdateService` `app/services/activitypub/process_status_update_service.rb:11` (snapshots via SnapshotConcern, re-fans with `update: true`) | inline→async |
| Remote inbound create | `ActivityPub::FetchRemoteStatusService` / `ProcessStatusService` (via `process_collection_service`) | async |

---

## Step 4 — Exhaustive dependent audit

### A. Feeds & timelines
- `app/lib/feed_manager.rb:49,67,76,90,102,117,448,486,502,520` — push/unpush/filter home, list, tags; reads visibility, reblog, account.
- `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` — local fan-out (see Step 3).
- `app/workers/feed_insert_worker.rb` — inserts a status id into one feed; loads `Status.find`.
- `app/lib/account_statuses_filter.rb:34-71` — builds account-profile timeline scopes off `account.statuses` + visibility.
- `app/lib/status_cache_hydrator.rb` — hydrates a cached status payload for feeds (uses `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS`).
- `app/workers/push_update_worker.rb` — pushes a status to a streaming timeline.
- `app/workers/scheduler/scheduled_statuses_scheduler.rb:17` + `app/workers/publish_scheduled_status_worker.rb:9-26` — publishes due `ScheduledStatus` via PostStatusService; resolves `thread`/`quoted_status` by `Status.find`.
- `app/lib/vacuum/feeds_vacuum.rb` — trims feed zsets (indirect; operates on status ids in redis).
- `app/models/account_conversation.rb` (`add_status`/`remove_status`, called from `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:166` and `status.rb:413`) — direct-message conversation feed.

### B. Federation (ActivityPub)
- `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:11` — outbound Create/Announce.
- `app/workers/activitypub/status_update_distribution_worker.rb` — outbound Update.
- `app/workers/activitypub/distribute_poll_update_worker.rb` — outbound poll Update.
- `app/lib/status_reach_finder.rb:7-117` — computes delivery inboxes from a status's interactions.
- `app/services/activitypub/fetch_remote_status_service.rb` & `app/services/fetch_remote_status_service.rb` — inbound status creation.
- `app/services/activitypub/process_status_update_service.rb:11-80` — inbound edits, snapshots, quote re-verify.
- `app/services/activitypub/verify_quote_service.rb` / `app/services/revoke_quote_service.rb` / `app/services/activitypub/fetch_remote_poll_service.rb` — quote/poll federation.
- `app/services/activitypub/fetch_all_replies_service.rb` + `app/workers/activitypub/fetch_all_replies_worker.rb` + `app/workers/activitypub/fetch_replies_worker.rb` + `app/workers/fetch_reply_worker.rb` + `app/workers/thread_resolve_worker.rb` + `app/workers/tagged_collection_resolve_worker.rb` + `app/workers/mention_resolve_worker.rb` — reply/thread/mention resolution (depend on `FetchRepliesConcern`).
- Serializers: `app/serializers/activitypub/note_serializer.rb`, `create_note_serializer.rb`, `announce_note_serializer.rb`, `update_note_serializer.rb`, `delete_note_serializer.rb`, `undo_announce_serializer.rb`, `add_note_serializer.rb`, `remove_note_serializer.rb` — serialize a status to AP.
- `FEDERATION.md` / `app/lib/activitypub/tag_manager.rb` (`uri_for`, used in `status.rb:199,426`) — URI identity contract.

### C. Notifications
- `app/services/notify_service.rb:67-91` — derives notification suitability from `target_status` (visibility, thread depth recursive SQL).
- `app/workers/feed_insert_worker.rb` / `LocalNotificationWorker` (mention/quote/update notifications, enqueued in `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:78-110`).
- `app/models/notification.rb` — polymorphic `activity` → Status (`status.rb:114`); `target_status` derivation.
- `app/workers/unfilter_notifications_worker.rb` — re-evaluates notifications referencing statuses.
- `app/workers/fasp/announce_trend_worker.rb` + `Status::FaspConcern` callbacks (`fasp_concern.rb:15-51`) — FASP lifecycle/trend announcements.

### D. Moderation
- `app/policies/status_policy.rb:4-88` — show/quote/reblog/favourite/destroy/update authorization.
- `app/lib/status_filter.rb:11-71` — block/mute/silence/policy filtering of a single status.
- `app/services/report_service.rb:9,35,64,83-93` — attaches `status_ids` to reports; forwards to remote inboxes.
- `app/services/approve_appeal_service.rb` — restores statuses tied to a strike/appeal.
- `app/models/status.rb:274-276` `reported?` — checks unresolved reports/strikes referencing the status id (drives `permanently?` in RemoveStatusService).
- Admin: `app/models/admin/status_filter.rb`, `app/models/admin/status_batch_action.rb`, admin controllers/views referencing statuses; `AccountWarning`/`Appeal`/`Report` carry `status_ids` arrays (not FKs — easy to orphan).
- `app/models/custom_filter.rb` / `custom_filter_status.rb` / `app/models/concerns/status/...` keyword filters applied to statuses.

### E. Account lifecycle
- `app/services/delete_account_service.rb:147,160-202,237,299-300` — `purge_statuses!` via `BatchedRemoveStatusService`, manual cleanup of mentions/polls/favourites/bookmarks/status_stats/index/cache; preserves reported statuses.
- `app/services/suspend_account_service.rb:71` — deletes `StatusTrend` rows; suspension also short-circuits fan-out (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:17`).
- `app/services/unsuspend_account_service.rb` — re-distributes / re-merges the account's statuses.
- `app/models/concerns/account/associations.rb:38-61` — `has_many :statuses`, `scheduled_statuses`, `status_pins`, `pinned_statuses`, plus favourites/bookmarks/mentions/conversations.
- `app/workers/scheduler/user_cleanup_scheduler.rb` — cleans up unconfirmed users (cascades to their statuses).

### F. Import / Export / Backup
- `app/services/backup_service.rb:29,37-42,140-183` — serializes `account.statuses`, favourited & bookmarked statuses into the AP outbox archive.
- `app/services/bulk_import_row_service.rb` — bookmark imports resolve to statuses.
- `app/lib/vacuum/backups_vacuum.rb` — trims old `Backup` archives (indirect).
- Export models `app/models/export.rb`, `bookmark.rb`, `favourite.rb` — CSV exports keyed on status ids.

### G. Search & trends
- `app/chewy/statuses_index.rb` + `app/chewy/public_statuses_index.rb` — ES mappings (`update_index` in `status.rb:72-73`); rely on `searchable_by`/`searchable_text`.
- `app/services/statuses_search_service.rb` + `app/services/search_service.rb` — query the index.
- `app/workers/add_to_public_statuses_index_worker.rb` + `app/workers/remove_from_public_statuses_index_worker.rb` + `app/workers/scheduler/indexing_scheduler.rb:30` — index maintenance.
- `app/models/status_trend.rb` + `app/models/trends/statuses.rb` (`Trends.tags.register` `post_status_service.rb:164`) — trending posts.

### H. Background maintenance
- `app/lib/vacuum/statuses_vacuum.rb:16-38` — hard-deletes old remote kept statuses, relies on FK cascade + `unlink_from_conversations!`.
- `app/workers/scheduler/vacuum_scheduler.rb:30` — schedules StatusesVacuum.
- `app/models/account_statuses_cleanup_policy.rb:59-156` + `app/services/account_statuses_cleanup_service.rb:16-20` + `app/workers/scheduler/accounts_statuses_cleanup_scheduler.rb:62` — user-configured auto-deletion of old statuses (`statuses_to_delete`, exception scopes on Favourite/Bookmark/StatusPin/poll/media).
- `app/workers/removal_worker.rb:7` — `RemoveStatusService` on `Status.with_discarded.find`.
- `app/services/remove_status_service.rb:16-168` & `app/services/batched_remove_status_service.rb:10-99` — teardown core (the code being reworked).
- `app/workers/unfavourite_worker.rb`, `app/services/favourite_service.rb` — favourite lifecycle counters.
- `app/lib/vacuum/media_attachments_vacuum.rb`, `preview_cards_vacuum.rb` — orphan cleanup of status-attached media/cards (note: `media_attachments` is `dependent: :nullify`, `status.rb:96`).

### I. Presentation / REST surface
- `app/serializers/rest/status_serializer.rb`, `shallow_status_serializer.rb`, `status_edit_serializer.rb`, `status_source_serializer.rb`, `scheduled_status_serializer.rb`, `filter_status_serializer.rb` — API output.
- `app/presenters/status_relationships_presenter.rb` — bulk favourite/reblog/bookmark/pin/filter maps (uses `Status.favourites_map` etc. `status.rb:361-379`).
- `app/lib/status_finder.rb:17` — URL→Status resolution.
- `app/serializers/activitypub/outbox_serializer.rb` + numerous controllers (`app/controllers/api/v1/statuses_controller.rb`, timelines, accounts, admin) — too many to be the model's contract, but all consume the above.

---

## Step 5 — Identity & visibility guards

When services act on a status, they confirm **(a) it exists, (b) is current/not-discarded, (c) is visible to the actor** through these layers:

- **Existence / current:** `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`) silently excludes discarded rows — so a plain `Status.find` already guards "not torn down"; teardown paths deliberately use `Status.unscoped`/`with_discarded` (`removal_worker.rb:7`, `report_service.rb:84,90`, `status.rb:370,402`). Workers rescue `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound` and no-op (`distribution_worker.rb:12`, `activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:16`).
- **Concurrency / race:** `with_redis_lock("distribute:#{id}")` in `DistributionWorker` (`:9`) and `RemoveStatusService` (`:22`); `FanOutOnWriteService#check_race_condition!` raises on null visibility (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:29-39`); `Status::SafeReblogInsert` (`safe_reblog_insert.rb:20-72`) refuses INSERT of a reblog whose parent is discarded at SQL level.
- **Visibility enum & helpers:** `Status::Visibility` — `distributable?`/`hidden?`/`public_visibility?`/`direct_visibility?` (`visibility.rb:27-35`); scopes `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` (`:12-14`). Used everywhere fan-out/reach/feed code branches.
- **Authorization:** `StatusPolicy#show?/quote?/reblog?/favourite?/destroy?/update?` (`status_policy.rb:4-37`) — checks `author.unavailable?`, mention/private/block/domain-block.
- **Per-viewer filtering:** `StatusFilter#filtered?` (`status_filter.rb:11`) wraps the policy plus block/mute/silence; `Status::ThreadingConcern.permitted_statuses_from_ids` (`threading_concern.rb:7-21`) rejects filtered statuses when walking threads.
- **Account-scope visibility:** `AccountStatusesFilter#initial_scope` (`account_statuses_filter.rb:34-46`) returns `Status.none` for unavailable/blocked, narrows visibility by follower/author/anonymous.
- **Federation reach guards:** `StatusReachFinder` gates interaction-derived inboxes on `distributable?` unless `unsafe:` (`status_reach_finder.rb:48,65-81,96-102`); `inboxes_without_suspended_for` drops suspended accounts (`:114-116`).
- **Suspension guard:** `FanOutOnWriteService` returns early if `proper.account.suspended?` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb:17`).

---

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

**🔴 Highest — break = data loss / orphans / federation desync**
1. `RemoveStatusService` + `BatchedRemoveStatusService` (`remove_status_service.rb:16`, `batched_remove_status_service.rb:10`) — they bypass callbacks (`delete_all`, `:34`) and replicate the `dependent:`/`unlink_from_conversations!` contract by hand. Any change to associations or the `discard_with_reblogs`/`unlink_from_conversations!` contract (`status.rb:400-415`) silently orphans mentions, polls, stats, index docs.
2. Soft-delete contract (`Discard::Model`, `deleted_at`, `default_scope` `:66,128`) + `Status::SafeReblogInsert` (`safe_reblog_insert.rb`) — if visibility/teardown changes how `kept`/`deleted_at` work, reblog-insert SQL and every `unscoped`/`with_discarded` call site (`removal_worker.rb:7`, `report_service.rb:84,90`, `status_reach_finder.rb:70`, `status.rb:370,402`) must be re-audited.
3. `Vacuum::StatusesVacuum` (`vacuum/statuses_vacuum.rb:16-38`) — relies on FK cascade + manual index/conversation cleanup; changing associations' `dependent:` or FKs orphans media (`media_attachments` is `:nullify`).
4. `DeleteAccountService#purge_statuses!` (`delete_account_service.rb:160-202`) — hand-rolled cleanup of stats/mentions/polls/index/cache that mirrors the model's `dependent:` declarations; must move in lockstep.

**🟠 High — break = wrong distribution / missed cleanup**
5. `FanOutOnWriteService` (`fan_out_on_write_service.rb`) + `FeedManager` (`feed_manager.rb`) — driven by visibility enum and `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS`; a visibility-storage change reshapes who gets the post.
6. `StatusReachFinder` (`status_reach_finder.rb`) — federation delete/undo reach depends on `distributable?` + `deleted_at` filter on reblogs (`:70`); wrong → ghost remote copies.
7. `ActivityPub::ProcessStatusUpdateService` (`process_status_update_service.rb:11`) — asserts `status.changed?` is false (`:12`) and depends on `SnapshotConcern`; edit/visibility changes can corrupt edit history.
8. Search indices `statuses_index.rb`/`public_statuses_index.rb` + `SearchConcern` (`search_concern.rb`) — `update_index` hooks (`status.rb:72-73`); teardown that skips callbacks must manually deindex (already done in 3 places — any new path must too).
9. `AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy#statuses_to_delete` (`account_statuses_cleanup_policy.rb:59-156`) — exception scopes reference Favourite/Bookmark/StatusPin/poll/media associations directly.

**🟡 Medium — break = stale counts / UI glitches**
10. Counter caches `increment/decrement_counter_caches` (`status.rb:160-161,479-493`) and `StatusStat` (`status_stat` `:396`).
11. `StatusCacheHydrator` + `Cacheable`/`CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS` (`status.rb:48-60,180-184`) — association renames break the cached payload.
12. Notifications: `NotifyService` (`notify_service.rb:67-91`), `Notification#target_status`, `LocalNotificationWorker`.
13. FASP: `Status::FaspConcern` (`fasp_concern.rb`) after_commit hooks.
14. Webhooks: `trigger_create/update_webhooks` (`status.rb:157-158,495-501`).
15. Moderation arrays: `Report`/`AccountWarning`/`Appeal` `status_ids` (`report_service.rb:35`) — non-FK integer arrays; deleted statuses leave dangling ids and `reported?` (`status.rb:274`) depends on them.
16. `BackupService` (`backup_service.rb`), serializers, presenters — output shape.

**Must re-verify after change:** every spec in Step 7; full delete→undelete federation round-trip; vacuum + account-cleanup + account-deletion orphan checks; reblog-of-discarded SQL guard; index in/out sync.

---

result:
I now have a comprehensive picture. Here is the full audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Mastodon — a Ruby on Rails 8 + (Vite/React frontend) ActivityPub-federated social server (`README.md:21`). Standard Rails layout under `app/`: `models/`, `services/` (business-logic command objects, the real workhorses), `workers/` (Sidekiq async jobs), `lib/` (plain service/utility objects), `policies/` (Pundit authorization), `serializers/` (REST + ActivityPub output), `presenters/`, `chewy/` (Elasticsearch indices), plus a Node `streaming/` server for real-time timelines.

**Central content model:** `app/models/status.rb` — `Status` is the post. Everything (feeds, notifications, federation, moderation, search, trends) revolves around it. It is an `ApplicationRecord` using soft-delete (`Discard::Model`, `deleted_at`) with a `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`status.rb:128`).

**Shared pieces it is built on:**
- Generic concerns: `app/models/concerns/cacheable.rb`, `paginable.rb`, `rate_limitable.rb`; `Discard::Model` (gem); `Paginable`.
- Status-namespaced concerns in `app/models/concerns/status/`: `visibility.rb`, `threading_concern.rb`, `snapshot_concern.rb`, `search_concern.rb`, `interaction_policy_concern.rb`, `fasp_concern.rb`, `fetch_replies_concern.rb`, `safe_reblog_insert.rb`.
- Satellite models: `StatusStat`, `StatusEdit`, `StatusPin`, `StatusTrend`, `Mention`, `Favourite`, `Bookmark`, `Poll`, `Quote`, `Conversation`, `MediaAttachment`, `PreviewCardsStatus`, `TaggedObject`, `CustomFilterStatus`.
- Service/lib helpers: `app/lib/feed_manager.rb`, `status_filter.rb`, `account_statuses_filter.rb`, `status_reach_finder.rb`, `status_finder.rb`, `status_cache_hydrator.rb`, `app/policies/status_policy.rb`.

---

## Step 2 — The Status contract & what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb:34`.

**Included concerns** (`status.rb:35-46`):
| Concern | file:line | Contributes |
|---|---|---|
| `Cacheable` | `concerns/cacheable.rb:3` | `cache_associated`/`with_includes`/`preload_cacheable_associations` — drives `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS` (`status.rb:48-60,180-184`) |
| `Discard::Model` | gem; `status.rb:36,66` | soft delete via `deleted_at`; `kept`/`discarded` scopes; powers `default_scope` (`status.rb:128`) |
| `Paginable` | `concerns/paginable.rb:3` | `paginate_by_max_id`/`paginate_by_min_id` (all timeline pagination) |
| `RateLimitable` | `concerns/rate_limitable.rb:3` | `rate_limit by: :account` (`status.rb:64`) — post rate limiting |
| `Status::FaspConcern` | `concerns/status/fasp_concern.rb:3` | after_commit announce new/update/delete/trend to FASP providers |
| `Status::FetchRepliesConcern` | `concerns/status/fetch_replies_concern.rb:3` | reply-fetch scopes + `should_fetch_replies?` |
| `Status::SafeReblogInsert` | `concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb:3` | overrides `_insert_record` to block reblogs of discarded statuses at SQL level |
| `Status::SearchConcern` | `concerns/status/search_concern.rb:3` | `indexable` scope, `searchable_by`/`searchable_text`/`searchable_properties` |
| `Status::SnapshotConcern` | `concerns/status/snapshot_concern.rb:3` | `has_many :edits` (StatusEdit, dependent: :destroy); `build_snapshot`/`snapshot!` |
| `Status::ThreadingConcern` | `concerns/status/threading_concern.rb:3` | `ancestors`/`descendants`/`self_replies`/`permitted_statuses_from_ids` (recursive CTE thread walk) |
| `Status::Visibility` | `concerns/status/visibility.rb:3` | `visibility` enum, `distributable?`/`hidden?`, `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` scopes |
| `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern` | `concerns/status/interaction_policy_concern.rb:3` | `quote_interaction_policy` composed_of; `quote_policy_for_account` |

**Associations other records hang off** (`status.rb:75-118`): `account` (`:77`), self-refs `thread`/`reblog`/`replies`/`reblogs` (`:82-93`), `favourites`/`bookmarks`/`mentions` (dependent: :destroy, `:89-94`), `media_attachments` (dependent: :nullify, `:96`), `poll`/`preloadable_poll` (`:80,116`), `quote`/`quotes` (`:98,118`), `conversation`/`owned_conversation` (`:79,87`), `status_stat` (`:115`), `trend`→StatusTrend (`:117`), `preview_cards_status` (dependent: :delete, `:112`), `tags` HABTM (`:110`), `tagged_objects` (`:97`), `notification` polymorphic `as: :activity` (`:114`), `edits` (from SnapshotConcern).

**Teardown contract** (what a teammate MUST know): soft-delete column `deleted_at` (`:66`); `discard_with_reblogs` cascades discard to reblogs (`:400`); `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!, prepend: true` runs before dependent-destroys (`:178,406`); counter caches on create/destroy commit (`:160-161,479-493`); webhooks + URI store + FASP all fire via `after_commit` (`:157-164`); `default_scope` hides discarded rows so most code never sees deleted statuses unless `unscoped`/`with_discarded`.

## Step 3 — Creation & distribution flow

| Layer | file:line | Inline / Async |
|---|---|---|
| Entry (local post) | `PostStatusService#call` `app/services/post_status_service.rb:42` | inline (controller) |
| Mentions/links/quote/tags parse | `process_status!` `post_status_service.rb:84-100`; `ProcessMentionsService`, `ProcessLinksService` | inline |
| Persistence | `@status.save!` in txn `post_status_service.rb:97-99` → model callbacks (`status.rb:157-174`: conversation, snowflake id, counter caches, store_uri, webhooks) | inline |
| Post-process fan-out trigger | `postprocess_status!` `post_status_service.rb:162-171` | enqueues async |
| → Local fan-out | `DistributionWorker` `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:8` → `FanOutOnWriteService#call` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` | **async** |
| → home/list/tag feed inserts | `FanOutOnWriteService` `:114-144` → `FeedInsertWorker` → `FeedManager.push_to_home/list` `app/lib/feed_manager.rb:76,102` | async |
| → local notifications (mention/quote/update) | `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:75-112` → `LocalNotificationWorker` | async |
| → public/hashtag streaming | `broadcast_to_public_streams!`/`broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!` `:146-163` (redis publish) | async (inside worker) |
| → federation out | `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker` `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:11` → `StatusReachFinder#inboxes` `:23` → `ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker` | **async** |
| Link preview | `LinkCrawlWorker` `post_status_service.rb:165` | async |
| Email digests | `process_email_subscriptions!` `:173` → `EmailDistributionWorker` | async |
| Poll expiry / quote request | `:169-170` → `PollExpirationNotifyWorker`, `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker` | async |
| Reblog path | `ReblogService` `app/services/reblog_service.rb` (mirrors: builds Status, DistributionWorker + ActivityPub::DistributionWorker) | inline→async |
| Edit path | `UpdateStatusService` `app/services/update_status_service.rb` + federated `ActivityPub::ProcessStatusUpdateService` `app/services/activitypub/process_status_update_service.rb:11` (snapshots via SnapshotConcern, re-fans with `update: true`) | inline→async |
| Remote inbound create | `ActivityPub::FetchRemoteStatusService` / `ProcessStatusService` (via `process_collection_service`) | async |
